summary: All four prescribed tests are present, substantive, and pass the litmus test against the implementation. Each new test fails meaningfully if the corresponding shared-helper invariant is broken (level prefix, whitelist enforcement before file open, length-bounding for i/w). The existing knownComponents_accepted test is correctly updated 14 -> 15 with 'inference-reset' inserted in the Phase-3.6 slot. Cleanup is symmetric via @After tearDown. No flakiness, no mock-only assertions, no excessive mocking.

tests_present.name: whitelistCount_is15
tests_present.notes: Asserts both ALLOWED_COMPONENTS.size == 15 AND 'inference-reset' in ALLOWED_COMPONENTS. Stronger than the spec required (which only asked for size). Litmus: deletes Phase-3.6 entry from ALLOWED_COMPONENTS -> both assertions fail. Passes.

tests_present.name: inferenceResetComponent_acceptedByAllLevels
tests_present.notes: Calls e/i/w in order, asserts file has exactly 3 lines, asserts each line begins with the correct level prefix and bracketed component. Litmus: drop level.name from buildLine head -> first prefix assertion fails. Drop write() body for INFO/WARN -> line count assertion fails. Order is asserted (lines[0]=ERROR, lines[1]=INFO, lines[2]=WARN), proving append order under shared mutex.

tests_present.name: unknownComponent_stillRejected_negative
tests_present.notes: All three levels exercised against 'inference-reset-x'; per-level boolean flags ensure each call site is verified independently (a single try/catch around all three would let one missing throw hide behind another). Final assertFalse(logFile.exists()) confirms whitelist check fires before File.appendText opens the path — proves the require() in write() runs before any I/O. Litmus: if write() omitted the require -> file gets created -> assertFalse fails.

tests_present.name: iAndW_lengthBoundingMatchesE
tests_present.notes: 600-char description and 300-char cause for both i and w. Asserts exact lengths (500 / 200) AND content homogeneity (all 'a' / all 'x') — content check defends against off-by-one in take(maxLen) producing a 500-char string that includes a stray separator char. Prefixes 'INFO [inference-reset] ' / 'WARN [inference-reset] ' verified. ' :: RuntimeException: ' confirmed via the substringAfter pivot. readLines() strips terminators so no trimEnd needed for the cause segment. Litmus: remove .take(maxLen) from sanitize() -> lengths assert 600/300 -> fails.

tests_present.name: knownComponents_accepted (updated)
tests_present.notes: Hardcoded count flipped 14 -> 15; 'inference-reset' added under a // Phase 3.6 comment, preserving the existing phase-grouped layout. After mass-writing to the file, asserts logFile.readLines().size == allowed.size — confirms every whitelisted component (including the new one) actually accepts via e().

coverage_notes: Concurrency / shared-mutex coverage for i/w is intentionally not added — task body explicitly says the single mutex inside write() is covered by the fact that all levels route through the same helper, and the test for write order (inferenceResetComponent_acceptedByAllLevels) already exercises three sequential calls hitting the same lock.

Sanitize-for-i/w (control-whitespace replacement) is intentionally not duplicated — task body cites descriptionSanitization_controlWhitespaceReplaced on e() as the reference test for the shared sanitize() path. Acceptable per task scope; would only be a gap if the helper had a level-conditional sanitize, which the implementation does not.
Rotation tests are intentionally absent per task body ('add rotation test not required').

Null-cause behaviour for i/w is not explicitly tested. Task body §Edge cases mentions 'добавь зеркальный тест если уровень доверия требуется' as optional. Existing nullCauseMessage_emitsEmptyTail covers the shared buildLine path through e(); since i/w delegate to the same buildLine with only level.name differing, the existing coverage is sufficient. Not flagged.

litmus_test_summary.notes: Each new test fails if the corresponding implementation invariant is removed: level prefix from buildLine, require() in write() before file open, .take(maxLen) in sanitize(), or 'inference-reset' from ALLOWED_COMPONENTS.
